questions.question: What were the three fatal weaknesses of RNNs?
options: Too many parameters, slow inference, big files

Sequential computation (1,024 tokens = 1,024 serial steps on massively parallel GPUs), vanishing gradients (information 50 tokens back is crushed through 50 non-linearities), and a fixed-width hidden-state bottleneck regardless of source length
No GPU support, no Python bindings, no docs
questions.explanation: "Attention Is All You Need" (2017) dropped recurrence entirely: every position attends to every other in parallel, training in one big matmul instead of N sequential steps.

questions.question: What is the fundamental compute difference between recurrence and attention?
options: O(N) serial depth vs O(1): an RNN can't compute h_5 before h_4, wasting 99% of parallel silicon, while the whole N×N attention matrix fills in one batched matmul with no step depending on another
RNNs can't run on GPUs at all
questions.explanation: In practice transformers train 5-10x faster per epoch at N=512, and the gap widens with length — until the O(N²) attention memory wall, which Flash Attention later addressed.

questions.question: What does the transformer trade away for parallelism?
options: Memory: attention scales O(N²) versus recurrence's O(N) — fine at 2K context, but 128K needs sliding windows, RoPE extrapolation, Flash Attention tiling, or linear-attention variants
questions.explanation: Transformers trade time for memory and then win the time back through parallelism — the memory bill comes due at long context.

questions.question: What is the inductive-bias shift from RNNs to transformers?
options: RNNs assume locality and recency; transformers assume NOTHING — every pair is an attention candidate, so they need more data to train well but scale further once they have it (Chinchilla formalized: enough tokens and a transformer always beats an equal-size RNN)
questions.explanation: Weaker priors + more data beats stronger priors + less — the recurring bet of the scaling era.

questions.question: What evidence shows the transformer is modality-general?
options: By 2026 the same block powers language (GPT-5, Claude 4, Llama 4), vision (ViT, DINOv2, SAM 3), audio (Whisper), biology (AlphaFold 3), and robotics (RT-2) — same architecture, different inputs
Each modality needed a new architecture
questions.explanation: One architectural bet changed every scaling curve in deep learning after 2017 — which is why this phase studies the block in such depth.
